cards.title: Type 2 Diabetes — Comprehensive BNYS Protocol

rows: 500mg TDS with meals. Additive with metformin.
16:8 TRE or 5:2 protocol. Not for insulin-dependent without supervision.
HPA axis, vagal tone, muscle glucose uptake
Surya Namaskar 12 rounds + Pranayama 30 min. ↓HbA1c ~0.5% RCTs.

pearls.label: 🟡 T2DM reversal — the 3-month window
pearls.text: T2DM is reversible in early-stage patients. DiRECT trial: 15kg+ weight loss → 46% remission at 1 year. β-cell function recovers when hepatic + pancreatic fat is depleted. BNYS intensive residential programmes show 60–80% medication reduction in motivated patients within 3 months. Document as "remission" (HbA1c <6.5% off medication ≥3 months), not "cure".

cards._legacyHtml:
Diagnosis (ADA)
FPG ≥7.0 · 2hPG ≥11.1 · HbA1c ≥6.5% · Random ≥11.1 + symptoms
BNYS targets
FPG 5.0–7.2 · 2hPP <10.0 · HbA1c <7.0%
Monitoring
HbA1c 3-monthly · HOMA-IR · Urine ACR · Lipids · eGFR
PillarInterventionEvidence/Dose
DietLow-GI Mediterranean or low-carb ★Carbs <130g/day, eliminate refined sugar, fibre 35+g/day
Exercise ★★Aerobic + resistance training150 min/week + 2× resistance. Post-meal walking 10 min ↓PPG 22%
Berberine ★AMPK activation (metformin equivalent in RCTs)500mg TDS with meals. Additive with metformin.
InositolInsulin second messenger restorationMyo-inositol 4g/day + D-chiro-inositol 400mg (40:1 ratio)
Bitter melon (Karela)Insulin-mimetic peptidesFresh juice 50–100ml/day or extract 1000mg TDS
Alpha-lipoic acidInsulin sensitiser + neuropathy600–1200mg/day. Reduces HbA1c ~0.5%
Intermittent fasting↓ Insulin resistance, β-cell recovery16:8 TRE or 5:2 protocol. Not for insulin-dependent without supervision.
Yoga therapy ★HPA axis, vagal tone, muscle glucose uptakeSurya Namaskar 12 rounds + Pranayama 30 min. ↓HbA1c ~0.5% RCTs.
🟡 T2DM reversal — the 3-month window
T2DM is reversible in early-stage patients. DiRECT trial: 15kg+ weight loss → 46% remission at 1 year. β-cell function recovers when hepatic + pancreatic fat is depleted. BNYS intensive residential programmes show 60–80% medication reduction in motivated patients within 3 months. Document as "remission" (HbA1c <6.5% off medication ≥3 months), not "cure".

cards.title: Metabolic Syndrome — Assessment & Full Protocol

alerts.label: IDF/AHA Harmonised Definition — 3 of 5 criteria
alerts.text:
1. Waist ≥94cm (M) / ≥80cm (F) [South Asian: ≥90/≥80]
    2. TG ≥1.7 mmol/L · 3. HDL <1.0 (M) / <1.3 (F) mmol/L
    4. BP ≥130/85 mmHg · 5. FPG ≥5.6 mmol/L

rows: Caloric restriction 500–750 kcal deficit, low-carb, HIIT
DASH diet, Mg glycinate 400mg, CoQ10 200mg, hibiscus tea

pearls.label: 🟡 Sleep + metabolic syndrome
pearls.text: Even 1 week of sleep restriction (5hr/night) induces insulin resistance comparable to 6 months of high-fat diet. Poor sleep ↑ ghrelin (hunger) ↓ leptin (satiety) → 300–500 extra kcal/day. Treating sleep apnoea reduces HbA1c, BP, and waist circumference independently of other lifestyle changes. Sleep is non-negotiable in metabolic medicine.

cards._legacyHtml:
IDF/AHA Harmonised Definition — 3 of 5 criteria
1. Waist ≥94cm (M) / ≥80cm (F) [South Asian: ≥90/≥80]
2. TG ≥1.7 mmol/L · 3. HDL <1.0 (M) / <1.3 (F) mmol/L
4. BP ≥130/85 mmHg · 5. FPG ≥5.6 mmol/L
ComponentRoot causeBNYS intervention
Central obesity↑ Visceral fat → ↑ FFA → insulin resistanceCaloric restriction 500–750 kcal deficit, low-carb, HIIT
Insulin resistance↓ GLUT-4, ↑ hepatic glucose outputBerberine 500mg TDS, inositol 4g/day, fasting (16:8)
Dyslipidaemia↑ VLDL (from IR), ↓ HDLOmega-3 2–4g/day, niacin (↑HDL), dietary fibre (psyllium)
Hypertension↑ Sympathetic tone, ↑ RAASDASH diet, Mg glycinate 400mg, CoQ10 200mg, hibiscus tea
Impaired FPGβ-cell dysfunction + peripheral IRCinnamon 1–3g/day, ACV 15ml before meals
🟡 Sleep + metabolic syndrome
Even 1 week of sleep restriction (5hr/night) induces insulin resistance comparable to 6 months of high-fat diet. Poor sleep ↑ ghrelin (hunger) ↓ leptin (satiety) → 300–500 extra kcal/day. Treating sleep apnoea reduces HbA1c, BP, and waist circumference independently of other lifestyle changes. Sleep is non-negotiable in metabolic medicine.

cards.title: Hypothyroidism — Functional Assessment & Protocol

rows: Low T4 + symptoms = refer for levothyroxine
Low T3 + normal T4 = poor T4→T3 conversion (check selenium, zinc, stress)
↑ rT3 = T4 shunted to inactive form: stress, caloric restriction, IR
Elevated = Hashimoto's → gluten-free + selenium 200µg + vitamin D
T4→T3 conversion; reduces anti-TPO by 50% in Hashimoto's RCTs
Thyroid hormone receptor binding, T4→T3 conversion
↑ T3 and T4 in RCTs of subclinical hypothyroid; ↓ cortisol → normalises rT3
Molecular mimicry (gliadin vs thyroid tissue) → ↓anti-TPO in 80% within 6 months

cards._legacyHtml:
MarkerLab NormalFunctional OptimalIf suboptimal
TSH0.5–4.5 mIU/L0.5–2.0 mIU/LTSH 2–4 + symptoms = subclinical hypothyroid → treat
Free T49–24 pmol/L15–23 pmol/LLow T4 + symptoms = refer for levothyroxine
Free T33.5–6.5 pmol/L5.0–6.5 pmol/LLow T3 + normal T4 = poor T4→T3 conversion (check selenium, zinc, stress)
Reverse T3<22 ng/dL<15 ng/dL↑ rT3 = T4 shunted to inactive form: stress, caloric restriction, IR
Anti-TPO antibodies<35 IU/mL<15 IU/mLElevated = Hashimoto's → gluten-free + selenium 200µg + vitamin D
Naturopathic interventionDoseMechanism
Selenium ★200 µg/day selenomethionineT4→T3 conversion; reduces anti-TPO by 50% in Hashimoto's RCTs
Zinc25–30 mg/day bisglycinateThyroid hormone receptor binding, T4→T3 conversion
Ashwagandha (KSM-66)300–600 mg twice daily↑ T3 and T4 in RCTs of subclinical hypothyroid; ↓ cortisol → normalises rT3
Gluten-free dietStrict eliminationMolecular mimicry (gliadin vs thyroid tissue) → ↓anti-TPO in 80% within 6 months

cards.title: Obesity — Naturopathic Assessment & Protocol

rows: Grade B — independent of calorie counting
Grade B — poor sleep sabotages all other interventions
Grade B — 1g before meals with water

cards._legacyHtml:
BMI classification
Normal 18.5–24.9 · Overweight 25–29.9 · Obese I 30–34.9 · II 35–39.9 · III ≥40
Asian cutoffs
Overweight ≥23 · Obese ≥27.5 (WHO Asia-Pacific)
Waist:height ratio
Best cardiometabolic predictor. Target <0.5
InterventionExpected effectEvidence
Caloric deficit 500–750 kcal/day0.5–1 kg/weekGrade A — cornerstone
Low-carbohydrate diet (<130g CHO)Superior short-term vs low-fatGrade A — ↓ insulin, hunger, TG
Time-restricted eating (16:8)3–8% body weight over 12 weeksGrade B — independent of calorie counting
Protein >1.2g/kg/dayPreserves muscle, ↑ satietyGrade A
Sleep optimisation (>7hr)↓ ghrelin, ↑ leptin sensitivityGrade B — poor sleep sabotages all other interventions
Glucomannan (konjac)~0.5 kg/month additionalGrade B — 1g before meals with water
